cross_references.rule: durability_rating_0_to_5 should reflect true moat strength (0-5 maps: 0=no moat, 1=weak, 2=okay, 3=good, 4=strong, 5=fortress) and correlate with ROIC and margin persistence in prior sprints
if durability_rating >= 4, ROIC should be elevated (>15%) and ROIC persistence score from prior sprints should support sustained competitive advantage

grounding_checks.description: All five moat source objects (switching_costs, network_effects, scale_or_cost_advantage, brand, regulatory_assets) must have non-null present and evidence fields
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: any_of(switching_costs.present, network_effects.present, scale_or_cost_advantage.present, brand.present, regulatory_assets.present) is null OR any_of(...evidence) is null

grounding_checks.description: If network_effects.present=true, type must not be 'none'; if present=false, type must be 'none'
If switching_costs.present=true, evidence length must be >= 50 characters
If network_effects.present=true, evidence length must be >= 50 characters
If scale_or_cost_advantage.present=true, evidence length must be >= 50 characters
If brand.present=true, evidence length must be >= 50 characters
If regulatory_assets.present=true, evidence length must be >= 50 characters
If durability >= 3, roic_persistence from agent-bundle should be >= 2 (moat requires sustained returns)
If durability >= 4, roic_persistence should be >= 4 (fortress moat requires very strong evidence of sustained competitive advantage)
If brand.present=true or scale_or_cost_advantage.present=true, gross_margin_trend from agent-bundle should not be 'declining' (price power or efficiency should show in margins)
If brand.present=true, pricing_power_score from agent-bundle should be > 0.4 (strong brand should show pricing power)
If any moat source is claimed as present, prior sprint unit_economics output should reflect defensible unit economics (consistent margin drivers)
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: any_moat_present=true AND unit_economics output is contradictory or vague

grounding_checks.description: If regulatory_assets.present=true, evidence must name specific licenses, permits, or regulatory barriers (not vague)
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: present=true AND evidence does not name specific regulatory asset

evidence_quality.prompt: Are the five moat mechanisms (switching costs, network effects, scale/cost advantage, brand, regulatory/toll bridge) grounded in specific mechanisms, with clear links to ROIC persistence or gross margin stability? Does the evidence cite actual competitive barriers, not generic features?
evidence_quality.score_5: All five moat types assessed with specific mechanisms tied to financial evidence (ROIC/margin/pricing power)
evidence_quality.score_3: Most moat types specific, 1-2 vague or unsupported by financial data
evidence_quality.score_1: Mostly generic moat descriptions without financial grounding or mechanisms unclear

completeness.prompt: Are all five Rule #1 moat categories (switching costs, network effects, scale/cost advantage, brand, regulatory/toll bridge) evaluated, even if some are marked absent? Is durability rating justified by the evidence presented?
completeness.score_5: All five categories assessed; durability rating clearly justified
completeness.score_3: Four of five assessed or rating partially justified
completeness.score_1: Fewer than four categories assessed or rating unjustified

durability_realism.prompt: Does the 0-5 durability rating reflect QVG methodology and calibrate realistically to the actual moat strength demonstrated? Is it consistent with ROIC persistence, gross margin trends, and competitive positioning from prior sprints?
durability_realism.score_5: Rating aligns perfectly with evidence; consistent with prior sprint data; believable per QVG framework
durability_realism.score_3: Rating mostly justified; 1-2 minor calibration concerns
durability_realism.score_1: Rating inflated/deflated vs evidence; contradicts prior data; implausible

red_flags.prompt: Is the output free of hallucinations, unsupported claims, and contradictions with prior sprints (unit_economics, industry)? Does moat assessment avoid marketing language and read like analyst research?
red_flags.score_5: Clean, no hallucinations; fully grounded; consistent with prior sprints
red_flags.score_3: 1 minor issue (slight overstatement or unverifiable detail)
red_flags.score_1: Contradicts prior data, contains fabricated moats, or marketing copy

framework_focus: Apply Rule #1 Five Moat Types (switching costs, network effects, scale/cost advantage, brand, regulatory/toll bridge). Evidence must link to ROIC persistence, gross margin stability, or specific competitive barriers. Rate durability 0-5 per QVG methodology — this score feeds the thesis sprint's quality_score_0_to_100 (moat is 25% of total quality score). Moat is the most analytically important sprint because it determines whether returns are sustainable.
notes: Moat sprint is highest-leverage for qualitative analysis. The durability rating (0-5) directly feeds thesis quality scoring and determines investment thesis feasibility. All moat claims must be mechanistically specific and grounded in financial persistence metrics from prior sprints. QVG framework prioritizes moat quality as proxy for competitive durability.
